title: ETS ordered_set table type — B-tree based
purpose: `ordered_set` tables maintain entries sorted by key using Erlang's term ordering. Unlike hash-based sets, ordered_set supports range queries and ordered iteration (first/next/last/prev). It's backed by a B-tree for O(log n) operations. OTP uses ordered_set for time-ordered indexes and priority queues.
task: Implement `EtsOrderedSet` using a `BTreeMap<Term, Term>` wrapped with appropriate concurrency control.

requirements.title: EtsOrderedSet implementation
acceptance: `EtsOrderedSet` wraps `Mutex<BTreeMap<TermKey, Term>>` where `TermKey` implements `Ord` using term comparison

requirements.title: TermKey wrapper for Ord
requirements.spec: Create a wrapper that implements `Ord` for Term using the existing term comparison infrastructure.
acceptance: Ordering matches Erlang's term ordering: number < atom < reference < fun < port < pid < tuple < map < nil < list < binary

requirements.title: Ordered iteration — first/next/last/prev
requirements.spec: Add ordered traversal methods to EtsOrderedSet that B-095 will expose as BIFs.
acceptance: `next(key) -> Option<Term>` returns the next key after the given key
`prev(key) -> Option<Term>` returns the previous key before the given key
Returns None at the boundaries (no next after last, no prev before first)
Test: insert 3 entries, verify first/next/last/prev traversal

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement concurrent read/write access in this brief — that's B-098
SHALL use standard library BTreeMap with a Mutex for thread safety (B-098 adds fine-grained locking later)
SHALL reuse the existing term ordering from B-007
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
